verdict.answer_first: B states the core answer immediately: "the port is already bound — so something is holding it"; A also starts well but adds more branches quickly.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits more cleanly to the main cause: "This is the most common case"; A surveys six causes and then shifts to environment-specific guesses.
verdict.fabrication: Both invent user-specific facts; A: "on your machine specifically — you run Node under a PM2/launchd bridge"; B goes further: "you run a PM2/launchd bridge (`mac-bridge-server`)".
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it gives targeted checks: "pm2 list", "launchctl list | grep -i node", and explains what it means if the PID comes back.
